title: Content Digests, Near-Duplicate Evidence, Change Classes, and History · Runtime Contract: Version Tombstone
summary: Canonical URLs, byte hashes, text similarity, DOM structure, and latest records are collapsed into one destructive duplicate decision. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go version tombstone increment in multi-stage versioned content evidence store. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Preserve observation history, supersession, tombstones, owner deletion, retention expiry, and report effects.
Classify content, template, metadata, accessibility, redirect, policy, parser, disappearance, and restoration changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Competency probe: Preserve observation history, supersession, tombstones, owner deletion, retention expiry, and report effects.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Version Tombstone model · Theory Version Tombstone, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Overwriting a map entry provides enough change history.
